description: A young Gideon and Nathaniel Malick attend their father's funeral. They are summoned to meet Daniel Whitehall, who tries unsuccessfully to recruit them away from Hydra’s religious traditions and sows seeds of distrust about their father's integrity during the stone-sacrifice ceremonies. The boys resist Whitehall, but his words prompt the start of their internal conflict.

transcript.line: Gideon, your father was a true believer. He'll be sorely missed.
Nathaniel and I will do everything we can to continue our father's work.
I'd expect nothing less from the Malick boys.
I already told them no, Nate.
We have to go see him.
We can't just ignore Daniel Whitehall.
He's an old man in a prison cell.
Who is still very powerful. And if he wants us to show...
I don't give a damn what he wants! Dad never believed in Whitehall's HYDRA, and we're not gonna start now. We're staying right here.
